title: Swelling in pregnancy: mild edema vs warning signs
excerpt: When ankle swelling is common, dietary sodium balance, and symptoms that may suggest preeclampsia — seek care promptly.
content:
## Mild swelling

Ankles and feet often swell later in pregnancy, especially in heat. Rest with legs elevated; comfortable shoes help.

## Eating and drinking

- Normal salt in home cooking is usually fine unless your clinician restricts sodium.
- Stay hydrated — dehydration does not prevent swelling.
- Potassium-rich foods (banana, beans) as part of balanced diet.

## Danger signs (possible preeclampsia)

Sudden facial swelling, severe headache, vision changes, upper abdominal pain, or rapid weight gain — go to emergency care.
